prep.summary: This is a team standup for the TF group — no attendees are listed, but the prep instructions call for two specific agenda items: announcing Matt Unsworth's departure and surfacing cross-cutting themes from sent emails this week. Matt was last mentioned in the Alan.ai sync (5-8) as a candidate being considered for the Solution Staff Engineer role alongside Neil Bowser, with leadership leaning against requiring a full panel interview for him. No prior TF Standup summaries exist in your notes.
tasks: Announce Matt Unsworth's departure — prepare a brief, factual statement covering his role, effective date, and any interim coverage or next steps the team needs to know.
Review your sent emails from this week and identify 2-3 cross-cutting themes to surface — look for patterns across Scan/Celonis alignment, mainframe modernization, and staffing movement.

Clarify the impact of Matt's departure on the Solution Staff Engineer hiring process — his candidacy was in flight, so address whether the role search changes or who absorbs his responsibilities in the interim.

Note any open items from the broader TF workstream that intersect with themes from the Aaron and Deepak threads — particularly E2E process visibility and the 'spreadsheet kill' initiative — in case they surface in discussion.
Be ready to field questions about organizational stability — the recent right-sizing actions have been a live concern across teams, and a departure announcement may prompt follow-up questions.
prep.title: TF Standup

prep.summary: This is the weekly P&C CTO Leadership staff meeting — the prep instructions override the default and call for a digest of EA off-site highlights pulled from the sent emails folder, organized by theme, plus any cross-cutting themes from the week. No sent emails folder is accessible here, so the synthesis below draws entirely from the five meeting summaries provided from the week of 5/12–5/19. The recurring threads across those meetings are: talent pressure and backfill urgency (Matt's departure, Vince as successor, mid-career architect retention), modernization sequencing and platform complexity (Guidewire/TRAD Plus, underwriting reinvention pillars, legacy PIF concentration), and AI tooling decisions under funding scrutiny (Scan AI vs. Celonis differentiation, agent assistance alignment, evaluation-driven development).
tasks: Present the EA off-site highlights organized into the three cross-cutting themes: talent and retention pressure, modernization and platform sequencing, and AI tooling and funding alignment.
Surface the Matt departure and Vince backfill decision as an open action item requiring a formal transition plan and leadership visibility.
Flag the Scan AI technical decision as time-sensitive — P&C funding is ready, but the Celonis differentiation case must be airtight before Dan Griffiths' expected challenge.
Raise the underwriting modernization framing (Risk Precision, Loss Avoidance, Operational Excellence) as a structuring lens for 2026–2027 investment conversations.
Note the TPM dashboard data gap (nothing current since Q2 2025) as an operational risk — domain health reporting is running on manual Excel workarounds ahead of upcoming outreach meetings.
prep.title: Weekly Staff – P&C CTO Leadership
